claim: Attaching outgroup ideas to a valued past state reduces resistance compared to a future state appeal, because the resistance stems from preference-style rather than content-disagreement.
People ascribe emotional minds to past selves and rational minds to future selves, treating change as moving from feeling-based to reason-based, with distinct motivational consequences.
Simple vote-counting of replication successes is misleading without meta-analytic tools to detect selection bias and p-hacking distortions.
Selective attention, though typically beneficial for learning, creates stable false beliefs when the selective mechanism repeatedly misses the true pattern.
Disrespect and cynicism form a bidirectional vicious cycle: experiencing disrespect generates cynicism which elicits further disrespect from others.
Liberalism is associated with greater neural sensitivity to conflict cues, reflecting heightened responsiveness to informational complexity.
People rely on visual portion cues to regulate intake rather than stomach signals; disrupting visual accuracy increases consumption without subjective satiation changes.
Socio-cognitive abilities (theory of mind, metacognition) decline with age while socio-affective capacities (empathy, compassion) remain stable or strengthen.
Social group membership (own-age bias) unconsciously shapes the interpretation of ambiguous perceptual input, revealing high-level effects on early perception.
Positive and negative emotion perception degrade differentially with distance from gaze; negative emotions are more vulnerable to peripheral-vision degradation.
Pre-exposure to misinformation production techniques (via gameplay) confers cognitive immunity, reducing susceptibility irrespective of demographics or cognitive style.

phenomenon: obligatory perceptual interpretation blocking pattern detection
claim: The syntactic parsing system obligatorily imposes linguistic structure on input, preventing detection of simpler statistical patterns that conflict with syntax.
Subtle linguistic cues reframing behavior as personal identity (versus action) increase motivation and actual behavior (voting) by invoking self-concept management.
Higher social class predicts unethical conduct across diverse domains; entitlement-related greed attitudes partially explain this disparity.
Humans rationally integrate noisy sensory input with semantic expectations using principles consistent with optimal statistical inference over corrupted signals.
Perceived intentional harm catalyzes a motivated blaming process (effort, persistence) distinct from merely rating the act as blameworthy.
Third parties motivated by self-interest promote cooperation in conflict by transforming the game structure; reduced risk to third parties increases intervention.
Postural expansiveness (body-opening in space) predicts romantic attraction because it communicates dominance, influencing rapid attraction judgments.
Higher-income individuals are less generous only when inequality is high; inequality-driven entitlement suppresses generosity.
Endorsement of false biological differences between racial groups predicts lower pain ratings and poorer treatment recommendations for black patients.
